title: Honey Garlic Chicken
tagline: Sticky, sweet, savory. The sauce caramelizes into something magical.
description: Chicken thighs glazed in a sticky honey garlic sauce. Crispy skin, tender meat, and a sauce you'll want to drink.
ingredients: Sesame seeds and green onions for garnish
steps: Season chicken thighs with salt and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a large oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat. Place chicken skin-side down. Cook 5-6 minutes until skin is golden and crispy. Flip and cook 2 minutes more. Remove to a plate.
Reduce heat to medium. Add garlic and cook 30 seconds until fragrant.
Whisk in honey, soy sauce, rice vinegar, and sriracha (if using). Bring to a simmer.
Return chicken to the skillet, skin-side up. Spoon sauce over the chicken.
Transfer to oven. Bake 15-18 minutes until chicken reaches 165°F, basting with sauce halfway through.
Let rest 5 minutes. Garnish with sesame seeds and green onions. Serve with rice.
notes: Crispy skin: Don't move the chicken while it's searing. Let it sit undisturbed to crisp.
Boneless option: Use boneless thighs and reduce oven time to 10-12 minutes.
The sauce: It thickens as it cools. Add a splash of water when reheating leftovers.
Make it hotter: Add more sriracha or a few dashes of red pepper flakes.
Meal prep friendly: Stores well for 4 days. Reheat in the oven to keep skin crispy.
